title: Should Shinji and Misato have invited Rei to live with them
display_title: Should Shinji and Misato have invited Rei to live with them
wikitext:
[]: Should Shinji and Misato have invited Rei to live with them? Post by InnerSphere » Sun Mar 16, 2014 5:00 pm I sometimes think about this. I'm not really sure if Misato know how bad Rei lives (since she was new in Tokyo 3 and we don't have any clue if she knew where was Rei's home, I think she didn't). But Shinji could talk about it to her, and they should have invited her. Probably she would refuse, just like she refused Asuka's invite to that party. Also some problems would obviously appear (Asuka wouldn't be really happy to live with Rei, I guess). But I think it would still be better for her.

[]: Post by Rei IV » Sun Mar 16, 2014 5:04 pm It would've interesting/neat idea but the whole "Three's a crowd" thing comes into play, not to mention the obvious hostilities with Asuka involved.

[]: Post by pwhodges » Sun Mar 16, 2014 5:12 pm I think that Rei's relationship with Gendou, and with Ritsuko (as the person who keeps her alive), would make it a complete non-starter.

[]: Post by BC Baron » Mon Mar 17, 2014 1:22 am Even if there wasn't enough extra space at Misato's place for all three pilots, perhaps they could've offered Rei one of the nearby apartments in the same building. I've read several fanfics which suggest that the rest of the building is completely vacant and it's tough to disagree with such a conclusion, since as far as I can recall, none of Misato's neighbors are ever shown. Having all three pilots residing in the same general location during their "off hours" probably would have been beneficial from both a logistical and morale/teambuilding standpoint. While it's true that the First Child might have rejected such an invitiation, I can't help but view the absence of even its suggestion as yet another example of Rei's day-to-day welfare being overlooked by Misato and/or the rest of the powers that be at NERV. Sadly, due to the simple fact that Rei never disobeys a direct order, Misato allows herself to believe that there are no problems with Unit-00's pilot and no supervision or support is required.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Mon Mar 17, 2014 3:35 pm It's not like Rei doesn't give a fuck about Rei, there are plenty of scenes that show her concerned for her wellbeing. Just watch when Ritsuko callously speaks of her death. Misato looks like she was about to break Ritsuko's jaw, but she had probably given up on slapping her at this point... But she took in Shinji because there was room for a guardian figure, with his dad being so obviously unengaged and him looking so obviously unhappy. He doesn't even have Asuka's superficially present communication with her stepmother. Misato doesn't need to be particularly sensitive to see the need there, and she really isn't all that skilled at parenting, she's just filling in because no one else will, but normally, you get to start out with a baby that eats a lot of time but basically just needs to have their bottle showed into their mouth regularly, and gradually graduate to more demanding tasks. With Rei, the situation is different: She already has a guardian, they regularly cheerfully talk to each other around NERV, Rei doesn't complain or create problems, and wouldn't it be sort of intrusive for Misato to stick her fingers in there, or at least look that way when you don't know the finer points of Rei's situation? It looks like someone's already meeting her needs if you're looking in from the outside, no suggestions a morale/teamwork measure is even necessary, or at least nothing she can give to Gendo as an excuse. On top of that, does it look like Misato's brand of just talking forcedly cheerful substanceless smalltalk at you to get you to bond with her would work with Rei? I assume she tried to be all friendly and personable the first few days, but after getting a few, "That won't be necessary"-ies and other assorted one-liners, she may jut have conclude that Rei isn't interested in going beyond a professional relation. Misato may have either shrugged and conluded that Rei is kinda weird, but seems to be okay with her job, or possibly blamed herself for somehow doing it wrong; She's not as confident as she looks. But there's nothing to suggest that she simply didn't care. And from a doylist perspective, having Rei involved in all those day by day funny scenes would probably have ruined the mystery and, more importantly, the sort atmosphere/aesthetic they were trying to communicate with her, of desolation, but also of an own, "third space" independent of what goes on in the katsuragi household. it would also have to have occurred before Shinji even arrived in Tokyo-3, since Misato arrived a while before him, so you'd start out with Rei already living at her place, which would screw up things storytelling wise.

[]: Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 17, 2014 4:11 pm It occurs to me that from a tactical standpoint even having two of the children live in the same place is probably a bad idea, much less with the field commander as well. If you're going to put the children all together they really should be quartered down in the geofront where they can be scrambled quickly, not some random apartment block in the city. I mean what if an angel shows up unexpectedly and just happens to blast Misato's place? Just shitty planning all around there, and putting Rei in the same place is begging for the worst to happen.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Mon Mar 17, 2014 4:21 pm NemZ wrote: It occurs to me that from a tactical standpoint even having two of the children live in the same place is probably a bad idea, much less with the field commander as well. If you're going to put the children all together they really should be quartered down in the geofront where they can be scrambled quickly, not some random apartment block in the city. I mean what if an angel shows up unexpectedly and just happens to blast Misato's place? Just shitty planning all around there, and putting Rei in the same place is begging for the worst to happen.

[]: Post by astrophys » Mon Mar 17, 2014 6:05 pm As others have noted, and I'lll agree: - Gendo would never allow it. - Having that many "essential" personnel in one place is not a wise move. Katsuragi and two pilots is already a huge risk as it is should anything happen. - On that note, I'll comment that gathering all the pilot candidates into one school is also a poor move in the event that the school or the shelter housing its students during an attack was destroyed… in that case, there goes your corps of readily prepared back-up pilots. Unless NERV has another set of reserves we never got to learn about, of course.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Mon Mar 17, 2014 6:19 pm ^They probably wanted the kids in Tokyo-3 in case Shinji and Asuka get flattened by the same angel in short order and swift replacements are needed.

[]: Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 17, 2014 6:37 pm Well really, if they were SERIOUS about this shit they'd have inducted all the local potential pilots into a training academy long ago and just given up on all this cover story bullshit. They should all have been training for years and living in group barracks down in the geofront with a professional training staff, not just tossing new recruits into combat and hoping for the best.

[]: Post by Chuckman » Mon Mar 17, 2014 8:20 pm Paul Verhoeven's Neon Genesis Evangelion.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 17, 2014 8:41 pm I'm getting a "we've seen this thread/discussion before" vibe. pwhodges wrote: I think that Rei's relationship with Gendou, and with Ritsuko (as the person who keeps her alive), would make it a complete non-starter.

[]: Post by ElMariachi » Tue Mar 18, 2014 3:43 am NemZ wrote: Well really, if they were SERIOUS about this shit they'd have inducted all the local potential pilots into a training academy long ago and just given up on all this cover story bullshit. They should all have been training for years and living in group barracks down in the geofront with a professional training staff, not just tossing new recruits into combat and hoping for the best.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Tue Mar 18, 2014 6:28 am ElMariachi wrote: Or maybe that was on purpose so SEELE could get rid of the pilots one the promised time arrive, imagine that the Harpies had to fight two or three pilots of Asuka's level in EoE? Well ANIMA already answered the question : the Harpies get completely curb-stomped.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 19, 2014 2:06 pm NemZ wrote: Well really, if they were SERIOUS about this shit they'd have inducted all the local potential pilots into a training academy long ago and just given up on all this cover story bullshit. They should all have been training for years and living in group barracks down in the geofront with a professional training staff, not just tossing new recruits into combat and hoping for the best.

[]: Post by NemZ » Wed Mar 19, 2014 4:57 pm You tell them something closer to the truth about 2nd impact, of course. How hard is it to keep the official story in place and only tell the chosen few a different rendition of the truth?

[]: Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 19, 2014 5:05 pm NemZ wrote: You tell them something closer to the truth about 2nd impact, of course. How hard is it to keep the official story in place and only tell the chosen few a different rendition of the truth?

[]: Post by NemZ » Wed Mar 19, 2014 5:38 pm So set it up out in the open, just don't tell the whole truth: it's an experimental boarding school funded directly by the UN, with campuses in a few major world powers. If successful the benefits of this new academic program will be extended to the rest of the world. Just completely bullshit what the actual program consists of, which shouldn't be especially difficult to sell in the pseudoscience realm of modern education anyway. But perhaps the better question is why they bothered with the asteroid story in the first place. Why not just say it was an alien being, the first of an expected wave of invaders that humanity needs to prepare to defend itself against. If they just tell that much and release a few images of a clearly huge, bipedal Adam running around the base it wouldn't take much to sell some sort of ender's game battleschool program.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 19, 2014 5:48 pm NemZ wrote: So set it up out in the open, just don't tell the whole truth: it's an experimental boarding school funded directly by the UN, with campuses in a few major world powers. If successful the benefits of this new academic program will be extended to the rest of the world. Just completely bullshit what the actual program consists of, which shouldn't be especially difficult to sell in the pseudoscience realm of modern education anyway. But perhaps the better question is why they bothered with the asteroid story in the first place. Why not just say it was an alien being, the first of an expected wave of invaders that humanity needs to prepare to defend itself against. If they just tell that much and release a few images of a clearly huge, bipedal Adam running around the base it wouldn't take much to sell some sort of ender's game battleschool program.

[]: Post by Chuckman » Wed Mar 19, 2014 6:59 pm Radiation from second impact makes it impossible for anyone born beforehand to pilot. That is to say, make shit up because no one can stop you anyway. Doesn't SEELE basically control everything to begin with?
